_note: Illustrative example. Brand, SKUs, competitors and figures are fictional placeholders to demonstrate the category-portfolio report structure — not a real market study. Replace with operator-supplied data; AI-scanned fields stay Hypothesis-grade until confirmed.

price_tiers.label: Mid · 600–1,200 PLN
price_tiers.audience: Mainstream upgraders replacing a first unit
price_tiers.force: Push + Anxiety (old-unit pain; fear of buying wrong)

diagnosis.title: No SKU in the fastest-growing premium sub-band
diagnosis.finding: The 1,200–1,800 PLN sub-band is where premium growth concentrates, yet the portfolio jumps straight from Aurora Pro (entry-premium) to Aurora Max (flagship), leaving the band empty. Premium Rival P owns it uncontested.
diagnosis.evidence: Own + competitor price listings across the three largest local marketplaces
diagnosis.implication: The single fastest-growing band is conceded by default; share captured here compounds as the category premiumizes.
diagnosis.recommendation: Position Aurora Pro into the 1,200–1,800 band, or introduce a Pro variant — do not leave the band to one competitor.

diagnosis.title: Volume concentrated in the shrinking entry tier
diagnosis.finding: Two of six SKUs sit in the entry tier, which is shrinking under a price war while the mid and premium tiers grow. The portfolio's center of gravity is in the wrong place for where the category is heading.
diagnosis.evidence: Category tier-trend read from marketplace assortment and price-drop frequency
diagnosis.implication: Effort and inventory anchored to a declining tier; margin erodes as the price war deepens.
diagnosis.recommendation: Harvest entry SKUs for margin, shift new investment to mid and premium.

diagnosis.title: A3 and A3 Plus cannibalize each other
diagnosis.finding: Aurora A3 and A3 Plus occupy the same price point and the same mid-tier position. Channels are forced to pick one; the two split demand and weaken both listings' review velocity.
diagnosis.evidence: Own product catalog + marketplace pricing (both live at near-identical price)
diagnosis.implication: Self-inflicted split of mid-tier demand; neither SKU builds the review mass that wins the band.
diagnosis.recommendation: Consolidate on A3 as the mid-tier hero; plan A3 Plus exit and clear inventory.

diagnosis.title: Aurora Pro may be stuck in a permanent-promo spiral
diagnosis.finding: Aurora Pro appears to carry a near-constant discount, which would erode its anchor price and make full-price sell-through impossible — a classic premium-killing spiral.
diagnosis.evidence: Observed promo-banner frequency on listing pages (no access to actual transaction price or margin)
diagnosis.implication: If true, the premium anchor is already damaged; if false, no action needed. Cannot tell without transaction-price data.
diagnosis.recommendation: Pull a 90-day transaction-price history; if permanent-discount confirmed, freeze promo and rebuild the anchor.

diagnosis.title: Entry SKUs over-concentrated on one marketplace
diagnosis.finding: Both entry SKUs lean on a single marketplace for visibility, while a meaningful share of first-time buyers in this category discover via bundled retail and telco offers where Aurora is absent.
diagnosis.evidence: Channel presence audit vs typical category purchase paths
diagnosis.implication: Concentration risk plus missed discovery surface; one marketplace algorithm change can wipe entry-tier visibility.
diagnosis.recommendation: Diversify entry distribution into bundle/retail; treat the single marketplace as one lever, not the whole shelf.

diagnosis.title: Flagship Aurora Max is invisible at launch
diagnosis.finding: Aurora Max is the brand's premium flagship but has no localized product page and zero reviews on the main marketplace — buyers searching the premium band cannot find it or trust it.
diagnosis.evidence: Localized PDP absent; review count = 0 (both directly verifiable)
diagnosis.implication: The SKU meant to establish premium brand altitude is silent exactly where high-consideration buyers decide.
diagnosis.recommendation: Build a localized PDP, seed expert reviews, and lead with technical media — do not discount the flagship.

portfolio.note: Aging entry unit in a shrinking, price-warred tier. No room to grow profitably; milk margin and prepare a successor.

fourP.product: Entry value unit; freeze spec, no new investment
fourP.price: Hold list; stop deep promo, protect margin
fourP.place: Keep marketplace listing; no new channel spend
fourP.promotion: None — let it run down; plan successor

portfolio.note: Defends the entry price band against Budget Rivals X/Y. Worth keeping as a price anchor, not worth scaling.

fourP.price: Match rivals −5–10%; price is the weapon
fourP.place: Diversify off the single marketplace (see L5)

portfolio.note: Mid-tier in a growing band with the clearest profit room. The hero SKU — consolidate the mid tier around it. → deep dive.

fourP.product: Mid-tier hero; lead on whole-home coverage
fourP.promotion: Scenario content: old-unit pain, room-by-room upgrade

portfolio.note: Cannibalizes A3 at the same price and position (see L3). No distinct job. Clear inventory and let A3 own the band.

fourP.place: Sell through existing channels, no replenishment

portfolio.note: Entry-premium with room to climb into the empty 1,200–1,800 band (see L1). Confirm the promo-spiral risk first (L4). → deep dive.

fourP.product: Stretch into the open premium sub-band
fourP.price: Rebuild anchor; freeze permanent promo pending data
fourP.promotion: Capability-led; prove the premium, don't discount it

portfolio.note: Flagship that should set brand altitude but launched invisible (see L6). Fix the digital shelf, then it can anchor the premium narrative. → deep dive.

fourP.place: Few launch channels, but each with full content
fourP.promotion: Localized PDP + seeded expert reviews + tech media

premium_subband_share.label: Aurora share in the 1,200–1,800 PLN sub-band
premium_subband_share.needed_from: Marketplace share estimate or sell-through data

entry_tier_trend.label: Entry-tier category volume trend (YoY)
entry_tier_trend.basis: Assumed −12% YoY from marketplace assortment thinning and price-drop frequency; not a measured figure

facts.fact: Premium air-purifier demand is growing faster than entry in the local market
facts.source_label: Illustrative placeholder — replace with a real cited source

cells.stop_rule: Pause if week-2 marginal ROI falls below 1.3x
cells.why: Validated mid-tier uplift and the strongest marginal ROI in the first spend steps

cells.stop_rule: Stop if review traffic lifts but conversion does not move by week 4
cells.why: Supports the same mid-tier hero after the validated search cell is funded
cells.identification: Confounding story: creator-content viewers self-select on purchase intent. Strategy: uplift modeled on last cycle's randomized creator-exposure split, adjusting for pre-exposure purchase propensity — identifiable because exposure assignment was randomized within the matched audience.

cells.measurement: Retail media platform lift read plus MMM calibration
cells.stop_rule: Cap spend when posterior ROAS lower bound drops below 1.3x
cells.why: Macro MMM supports retail media and Aurora Pro has the margin to absorb spend

cells.tau_basis: Category benchmark for PDP-led premium content uplift; no local test run yet
cells.measurement: Before/after PDP conversion with traffic mix guardrail
cells.stop_rule: Do not scale media until PDP conversion clears the premium benchmark
cells.why: Cheap enough to unlock Aurora Pro before larger paid media

cells.stop_rule: Stop if expert-review traffic does not lift premium PDP sessions by week 4
cells.why: Validated uplift exists, but reach is smaller than Aurora A3

cells.needed_from: Launch-market paid-search test — blocked until Aurora Max has a localized PDP (see L6)
cells.flight: Blocked until PDP localization is live
cells.stop_rule: Keep blocked until validation data exists
cells.why: Search demand exists but the flagship digital shelf is not ready

cells.stop_rule: Stop if bundle lift shifts units from higher-margin Aurora A3
cells.why: Hold SKU gets limited defensive spend only while ROI clears the floor

hte_validation.method: DR learner on randomized holdout summaries

labels.footer_note: Illustrative example — fictional data; every real number must be sourced, assumed, derived, or missing
